analysis: This week has 295 tasks due across 10 projects, with 123 tasks already overdue from previous weeks. That's a combined load of 418 items the team needs to manage.
🌧️ **Weather risk:** 1 day this week is likely too bad for outdoor work. 83 outdoor tasks are due this week — the PMs should plan around the weather and front-load outdoor work on the clear days.
⛔ **5 tasks are blocked** by overdue dependencies. These can't move until their prerequisites are done. The PMs responsible should treat those blockers as their #1 priority this week.

💰 **Payment chain alert:** 38 payments across all projects are effectively blocked because the work that needs to happen before them is overdue. Even though the payment dates look fine in the schedule, these payments CANNOT happen until the prerequisite work is done.
Of those, 5 are **client income payments** ($75,867). This is money that won't come in until the blocking work is cleared.
33 are **sub/material payments** ($39,360) — the work isn't done so these shouldn't be paid yet either.
🚨 **Income at risk:** 5 client payments ($75,867) are blocked by overdue work. Do NOT delay the tasks that lead to these payments — they're your path to cash.
**Katie Holmes & Sean Springs** has the heaviest load: 50 tasks due. With 10 already overdue, the PM needs to ruthlessly prioritize.
**Kyle Aubry - Project 01** has the heaviest load: 38 tasks due. With 25 already overdue, the PM needs to ruthlessly prioritize.
**Stephanie and Marc Felton** has the heaviest load: 37 tasks due. With 17 already overdue, the PM needs to ruthlessly prioritize.
